content: Research and portfolio demonstration of default-risk scoring using public repayment data and alternative-data-inspired features.
Production lending, automated rejection, or real borrower scoring without consent, governance, regulatory review, and true out-of-time validation.
The runnable dataset was not genuine mobile-money data; it was an open credit-default proxy dataset.
True USSD, airtime, and mobile-money records would require strict minimization, consent, encryption, access control, and retention limits.
Group diagnostics were performed only for available proxy groups and did not constitute a complete fairness audit.
A deployed model would need drift monitoring, calibration monitoring, adverse-action logging, and periodic revalidation.
